identificationModule.briefTitle: De-Implementation of Low-value Testing in Patients Undergoing Low-Risk Surgery
identificationModule.officialTitle: De-Implementation of Low-value Testing in Patients Undergoing Low-Risk Surgery

responsibleParty.investigatorTitle: Research Professor of Cancer Quality Improvement

collaborators.name: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality (AHRQ)
Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan Foundation

descriptionModule.briefSummary: This trial aims to evaluate the effectiveness of a multi-level, multi-component de-implementation strategy to reduce unnecessary preoperative testing. Sixteen Michigan Value Collaborative (MVC)/Michigan Surgical Quality Collaborative (MSQC) sites in Michigan will implement several tools that have been proven to reduce unnecessary testing at a single site, including clinician education, a decision aid, audit and feedback on performance, and a pay-for-performance incentive. The researchers believe that, through the use of these strategies, there will be a significant reduction in unnecessary preoperative testing during the intervention.

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Using a stepped wedge design, sixteen Michigan Value Collaborative (MVC)/Michigan Surgical Quality Collaborative (MSQC) sites in Michigan will implement a multi-level, multi-component de-implementation strategy and collect information on the use of unnecessary preoperative testing.

armGroups.label: Wave 1
armGroups.description:
Each wave will include usual care, preparation (8 weeks before intervention), active de-implementation interventions, and sustainment phases.

Intervention is Year 1 Quarter 2

armGroups.label: Wave 2
armGroups.description:
Each wave will include usual care, preparation (8 weeks before intervention), active de-implementation interventions, and sustainment phases.

Intervention is Year 1 Quarter 3

armGroups.description:

armGroups.description:

armGroups.description:

interventions.name: Active de-Implementation
interventions.description: During active de-implementation (3 months), the local team will distribute the strategy components (i.e., clinician education, decision support, evaluation, consensus building around perioperative testing pathways, facility-specific feedback, and a pay-for-performance incentive) at the local site.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Number of tests within 30 days of surgery not associated with an emergency department visit
primaryOutcomes.description:
Only tests relating to three index procedures; breast lumpectomy, laparoscopic cholecystectomy, and inguinal hernia repair

Data collected using MVC/MSQC database

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Number of unnecessary preoperative testing before low-risk surgery at MVC/MSQC sites not participating in the trial
secondaryOutcomes.description: Example related operations: laparoscopic hysterectomy, knee arthroscopy
Using the MVC claims data for total spending amounts

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Number of unnecessary preoperative testing before low-risk surgery at trial sites
secondaryOutcomes.description: Example related operations: laparoscopic hysterectomy, knee arthroscopy

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Site

Inclusion Criteria:

* Over 30% of testing rates on at least one of the three index procedures (breast lumpectomy, laparoscopic cholecystectomy, and inguinal hernia repair)

Exclusion Criteria:

* none

Surgical participants must be over 18 years old.

locations.facility: University of Michigan and other Michigan Surgical Quality Collaborative sites
